key_issues: CRITICAL: the web-server check-false (#rx"/web-server/") is vacuously-passing — no swept module imports web-server, and the mandated racket/tcp teeth check only fires the banned-module loop, never the web-server assertion. A typo regex ships green forever. Fix: add a second teeth injection `(require web-server/http)` into one module, confirm the web-server check goes RED, then revert; record it in Decisions.

CRITICAL: M10 coverage is not permanently proven. The only mandatory non-vacuity guard (#rx"core/main\.rkt") sits under the transport.rkt/M6 branch; in-memory.rkt/M10 is a separate barrel branch whose reachability is only an OPTIONAL assertion plus a transient mutation. Promote a `visited-has? #rx"in-memory\.rkt"` (or racket/async-channel presence) guard to MANDATORY so both M6 and M10 subtrees are non-vacuous.
SUGGESTED: progress.md line numbers (:109/:110/:338) are accurate now but sibling items 020/021/023 edit the same section; drift risk. Anchor edits by the quoted box text, not the bare line number.

SUGGESTED: racket/port is in the ban list; the two new transitive imports (racket/generic, racket/async-channel) must be RUN and observed green, not assumed. Spec should state that a banned-hit via async-channel is a real portability finding to escalate, not a test bug to suppress by weakening the ban list.

Confirmed-correct (no change): web-server separate-assertion rationale + regex, single-root base-dir threading via (path-only root), core/main.rkt path-presence edge for M6, scope of progress.md edits (only :109/:110, leaves :106/:107/:108/:111 and Stage-S3 status to items 020/021/023), no roadmap edit (no materialized table), async-channel correctly not banned.
